discovery.run_summary: Broad tape is modestly risk-on with SPY up, VIX crushed below 15, and META leading mega-caps higher. The standout dislocation is biotech: XBI down sharply against a green tape, dragging multiple mid-cap biotechs lower with no company-specific 8-Ks. Also notable: Mercury Systems continuing a multi-day decline against a green defense sector.

notable_index_moves: XBI down ~3.2% while SPY/QQQ green — biotech-specific selloff, not broad healthcare (XLV only -0.82%)
VIX collapsing >5% to 15 handle — complacency building
META +6% on renewed AI enthusiasm; NVDA +4% on SK Hynix debut halo
IWM red while large-caps green — small-cap weakness under the surface

discoveries.name: Beam Therapeutics Inc.
discoveries.setup: Gene-editing biotech selling off inside a broader XBI decline
discoveries.thesis: BEAM is down materially on essentially average volume (1.09x) with no 8-K, no scheduled earnings, and no company-specific news surfaced. The move is consistent with high-beta gene-editing names amplifying an XBI down-day; CRSP moving similarly reinforces this is a sub-sector unwind, not idiosyncratic. Magnitude looks proportional to XBI beta.
discoveries.what_confirms: If a policy/FDA headline for gene-editing or a peer readout (VERV, NTLA) surfaces explaining the coordinated CRSP+BEAM move.
discoveries.what_kills: If a BEAM-specific pipeline setback (e.g., clinical hold, trial pause) emerges in filings later today or tomorrow — that would reclassify as UNDERDONE-down.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When two same-sub-sector high-beta names (BEAM, CRSP) move down in near-lockstep with no filings, treat as sub-sector unwind rather than idiosyncratic — chase the sub-sector explanation before the single-name one.
discoveries.catalyst: Sympathy with XBI selloff and gene-editing sub-sector weakness
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals, average volume, and CRSP moving in parallel point to sub-sector rotation rather than single-name news.
research_pointers: Check VERV, NTLA, EDIT for parallel moves confirming gene-editing sub-sector unwind
Scan FDA/CBER announcements today for gene therapy policy signals
Check for any large biotech-fund redemption flows or ETF rebalance notes

discoveries.name: Axsome Therapeutics, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Commercial-stage biotech down sharply on BELOW-average volume with no filings
discoveries.thesis: AXSM is down materially on only 0.67x average volume with no 8-K and no scheduled earnings. A -6% print on light volume in a commercial-stage name (not a binary clinical name) inside an XBI-wide selloff typically overshoots because there are no natural buyers on a quiet-tape biotech down-day. Absent a catalyst, this is beta-driven overshoot rather than fundamental repricing.
discoveries.what_confirms: If AXSM retraces meaningfully as XBI stabilizes over the next 1-3 sessions, or if peers with similar profiles (revenue-stage CNS biotechs) show the same pattern.
discoveries.what_kills: A subsequent 8-K revealing Auvelity/Symbravo prescription weakness, label issue, or competitive setback — or if volume spikes late-day on a leaked headline.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Sub-average-volume biotech drops inside sector selloffs often overshoot for commercial-stage names because the marginal seller is a sector-ETF forced hand, not an informed holder.
discoveries.catalyst: Apparent sympathy with XBI selloff; no company-specific catalyst identified
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals is empty and volume is 0.67x average — a fundamental catalyst would typically bring volume, not suppress it.
research_pointers: Verify Auvelity/Symbravo weekly scripts (IQVIA/Symphony) haven't rolled over
Check for any analyst downgrades or channel-check notes today
Compare against other commercial-stage CNS biotechs (SAGE, JAZZ) for sympathy pattern

discoveries.name: Mercury Systems Inc
discoveries.setup: Defense mid-cap extending a multi-day decline against a green defense tape
discoveries.thesis: MRCY is down materially today and down substantially over five sessions while ITA is roughly flat and XLI is green. Volume is below average (0.64x), which is unusual for a name in a fifth day of decline — typically capitulation brings volume. No 8-K, no news surfaced. Cannot articulate either direction at conf 3+: the isolation from sector suggests idiosyncratic bad news not yet visible (bearish), but the low volume and absence of any filing after multi-day weakness argues against a fresh catalyst (potentially exhausted).
discoveries.what_confirms: An 8-K or contract-loss/DoD-program headline appearing in the next 48 hours would confirm a bearish idiosyncratic thesis.
discoveries.what_kills: A snap-back rally on no news would suggest the multi-day weakness was purely technical/positioning.
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — sustained underperformance vs. defense peers with no visible trigger
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals combined with divergence from ITA suggests idiosyncratic pressure but source isn't identifiable from available data.
research_pointers: Check DoD contract awards database for recent MRCY losses to peers (KTOS, CACI)
Look for any short-seller reports or downgrades in past week
Check insider Form 4 filings for recent selling

discoveries.name: BridgeBio Pharma, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Recent biotech winner giving back part of a strong 5-day run inside sector selloff
discoveries.thesis: BBIO is down today but still up substantially over five sessions. Elevated volume (1.32x) fits a partial profit-take on a name that ran hard, amplified by XBI weakness. Magnitude looks proportional given both the sector down-day and the extended prior run.
discoveries.what_confirms: If BBIO stabilizes above pre-run levels while XBI stabilizes, confirming this was profit-taking not thesis-breaking.
discoveries.what_kills: If BBIO gives back the entire five-day gain and keeps going, an Attruby (acoramidis) commercial or competitive concern (BMY Camzyos, PFE tafamidis dynamics) may be surfacing.
discoveries.catalyst: Profit-taking on recent run compounded by XBI selloff
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Positive 5-day return combined with elevated volume on a down day is the signature of profit-taking, not fresh negative news.
research_pointers: Track Attruby TTR-CM prescription trends vs. Pfizer's tafamidis
Watch for any competitor readouts (Alnylam, Ionis TTR programs)

discovery.no_signals_note: Primary observation of the session: biotech-specific selloff (XBI -3.2%) inside an otherwise risk-on tape (SPY +0.43%, VIX -5%). Multiple biotech movers share the pattern of no 8-K, no earnings, and the sub-sector explanation dominates. Michael should look for the underlying biotech catalyst (policy, macro, or peer readout) rather than treat these as independent single-name signals.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: The AI announcements today (Hugging Face open-source commentary, SK Hynix IPO, OpenAI GPT-5.6 launch, OpenAI leadership change, Atlas browser shutdown, Musk/Anthropic hosting comments) do not have plausible direct linkage to the moving stocks in the market data. The movers are almost entirely biotech names (BEAM, CRSP, AXSM, BBIO, PTCT) and one defense electronics name (MRCY). None of the AI announcements target drug discovery, gene editing, or defense electronics in a way that would explain today's moves. Biotech sector weakness on a given day is far more likely driven by clinical/regulatory/rate news than by GPT-5.6 or open-source AI commentary. MRCY's -14% five-day drawdown suggests a company-specific or defense-budget catalyst, not AI news. No actionable AI-driven mispricing thesis identified from this dataset. Confidence in 'no linkage': 4/5.
